title: Assistant-Guided Decoding
existing_short_definition: Speculative decoding where a small 'assistant' model drafts tokens for the large target model to verify — generic term for the technique popularized by Medusa and EAGLE.

title: Beam Search
existing_short_definition: A decoding method that tracks the top-B candidate sequences in parallel and returns the highest-scoring complete sequence. Used heavily in translation, rarely in chat.
existing_code_example:
# Beam search in HuggingFace Transformers
outputs = model.generate(
    input_ids,
    num_beams=5,
    max_new_tokens=200,
    early_stopping=True,
    no_repeat_ngram_size=3,
)

title: Constrained Decoding
existing_short_definition: Generation guaranteed to follow a formal grammar — JSON schema, regex, or BNF — by masking disallowed tokens at each step.

title: Contrastive Decoding
existing_short_definition: A decoding method that subtracts a smaller 'amateur' model's logits from the main model's logits to suppress generic predictions and amplify expert signal.

title: Deterministic Decoding (Seeded Generation)
existing_short_definition: Using temperature = 0 (or a fixed random seed) so the model produces the same output for the same input across runs.

title: Diverse Beam Search
existing_short_definition: A beam search variant that penalizes similarity between beams so the returned N candidates are meaningfully different from each other.

title: DRY Sampling
existing_short_definition: 'Don't Repeat Yourself' sampling — a repetition-penalty variant that penalizes repeated n-grams rather than individual tokens, reducing loops without over-suppressing common words.

title: EAGLE Decoding
existing_short_definition: A speculative-decoding method that uses a lightweight auto-regressive head attached to the target model, achieving 3–4x speedups with minimal quality loss.

title: Entropy-Based Decoding
existing_short_definition: Decoders that adapt sampling parameters (temperature, top-p, cutoffs) based on the current step's distribution entropy — more creative when uncertain, more conservative when confident.

title: Frequency Penalty
existing_short_definition: OpenAI-style sampling parameter that reduces a token's logit proportional to how often it has appeared in the generated text so far.

title: Greedy Decoding
existing_short_definition: The simplest decoding method: at every step pick the single highest-probability token. Deterministic and fast, but often produces repetitive output.
existing_code_example:
# Deterministic mode
response = client.chat.completions.create(
    model='gpt-4o-mini',
    messages=msgs,
    temperature=0,  # greedy
    seed=42,        # further reproducibility
)

title: Guidance (Microsoft)
existing_short_definition: A Python library for structured LLM generation that interleaves fixed template text with variable generation, guaranteeing adherence to format.

title: Logit Bias
existing_short_definition: A per-token adjustment that adds (or subtracts) a constant from specific token logits at inference, forcing or forbidding certain outputs.
existing_code_example:
resp = openai.chat.completions.create(
    model='gpt-4o',
    messages=[{'role':'user','content':'Is this spam?'}],
    logit_bias={'9642': 100, '2360': 100, '2822': 100},  # yes/no/N/A
    max_tokens=1)

title: Log Probability (Logprobs)
existing_short_definition: The natural logarithm of the probability a model assigns to each generated token — a core signal for confidence, eval, and retrieval.

title: Min-P Sampling
existing_short_definition: A sampling method that keeps tokens with probability above a fraction (p_min) of the most likely token's probability, adapting the cutoff to confidence.

title: Mirostat
existing_short_definition: A sampling method that dynamically adjusts top-k to maintain a target output perplexity (surprise level) across the generation.

title: Multinomial Sampling
existing_short_definition: Drawing the next token by sampling from the model's full probability distribution over the vocabulary, weighted by predicted probabilities.

title: Outlines (Structured LLM)
existing_short_definition: An open-source library for constrained decoding against regex, JSON schema, and context-free grammars via efficient automaton-based token masking.

title: Perplexity
existing_short_definition: The exponentiated average negative log-likelihood of a sequence under a language model — lower means the model finds the sequence more natural.

title: Presence Penalty
existing_short_definition: A sampling parameter that applies a flat logit reduction to any token that has already appeared at least once, encouraging topic diversity.

title: Reasoning Token / Thinking Budget
existing_short_definition: A parameter that caps how many tokens a reasoning model spends on internal reasoning before producing its final answer.

title: Repetition Penalty
existing_short_definition: A sampling modifier that reduces the probability of tokens already present in the context, discouraging the model from repeating itself.

title: Stop Sequences
existing_short_definition: Strings that, when generated, cause the model to halt output — used to delimit structured responses or prevent over-generation.

title: Temperature
existing_short_definition: A setting that controls how random or creative an AI's responses are.

title: Top-k Sampling
existing_short_definition: A decoding strategy that samples the next token from only the top K most-likely candidates, ignoring the rest.
existing_code_example:
# Top-k in Ollama (via REST API)
# curl localhost:11434/api/generate -d '{"model":"llama3","options":{"top_k":40}}'

# In vLLM Python:
from vllm import SamplingParams
sp = SamplingParams(top_k=40, top_p=0.9, temperature=0.7)

title: Top-p (Nucleus) Sampling
existing_short_definition: A decoding strategy that samples from the smallest set of tokens whose cumulative probability exceeds a threshold p — typically the best default for creative text generation.
existing_code_example:
# Top-p sampling in OpenAI
response = client.chat.completions.create(
    model='gpt-4o-mini',
    messages=msgs,
    temperature=0.7,
    top_p=0.9,
    # top_k is not OpenAI-exposed; they apply it internally
)
# In llama.cpp / Ollama:
# curl localhost:11434/api/generate -d '{"model":"llama3","options":{"top_p":0.9,"temperature":0.7}}'

title: Typical Sampling
existing_short_definition: Sampling tokens whose information content is close to the distribution's expected entropy — biasing away from both too-predictable and too-surprising tokens.
